title: Halmia × lobata

intro: Halmia × lobata, also known as the Halmia or Lobelia, is a flowering plant in the bellflower family, Campanulaceae, characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help ease congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Halmia × lobata include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Halmia × lobata is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity and side effects.
